arts.ttl: Celtic hero Kyogo Furuhashi's difficult start to Rennes career continues
arts.sum: With just two appearances for Rennes since Kyogo’s big £10m move, the Hoops icon has suffered an injury setback that is all too familiar to the Celtic fans. The Japan star suffered a setback just minutes into his second substitute appearance for Rennes after needing to be taken off to have his infamous shoulder looked at after appearing to injure it again as boss Habib Beye clears up what happened to the former Celtic man.

arts.ttl: 'A joke' - Celtic cult hero named as WORST Ronaldo has played with
arts.sum: 'A joke' - Celtic cult hero named as the worst Ronaldo has played with.

arts.ttl: James McFadden says 'what many people thought' would happen to Jota at Celtic hasn't happened
arts.sum: Jota netted against Dundee United – it was his first goal at Celtic Park since returning to the club in January from Ligue 1 side, Rennes. Days after the disappointment of the Bayern Munich game, Celtic beat Dundee United 3-0 to push closer towards winning another Premiership title.

arts.ttl: Man United transfer news: Joshua Kimmich update provided by Bayern Munich chief amid links
arts.sum: Bayern Munich president Herbert Hainer provides an update on the future of Joshua Kimmich, whose contract is due to expire at the end of the season.

arts.ttl: Daizen Maeda lands Tottenham transfer pitch as Ange Postecoglou needs star to set tone for his Celtic mantra
arts.sum: The forward has been in scintillating form this season with Europe on alert after his top form in the Champions League

arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers tells Martin O'Neill why he left Celtic in Amazon interview, his response is brutal
arts.sum: O’Neill was quick to correct the current Celtic manager by saying, ‘no, you just wanted to leave’, before that drew an awkward laugh from Rodgers. O’Neill then interrupted by pointing at Rodgers who laughed: “No, you just wanted to leave, young man.

arts.ttl: Max Eberl says it would be “dream” to have Florian Wirtz at Bayern Munich after 0-0 draw a Bayer Leverkusen
arts.sum: Bayern Munich battled to a 0-0 draw against Bayer Leverkusen at the BayArena to gain a valuable point away from home and cement an eight point gap at the top of the Bundesliga table. Speaking after the 0-0 draw, board member for sport commented on Bayern’s links to Wirtz and the recent comments made by former club president Uli Hoeneß, saying that Bayern definitely wants to sign him.

arts.ttl: Joshua Kimmich admits what Celtic fans did at Parkhead spooked Bayern Munich
arts.sum: With Celtic fans now allowed to attend the away tie at the Allianz Arena, Bayern Munich’s Joshua Kimmich has shared how the Hoops fans inside Parkhead last Wednesday spooked his teammates towards the end of the game. Kimmich told The Mirror [print edition page 62], “We managed to keep the crowd quiet but towards the end, it did get really loud, we lost a bit of control and immediately the fans were there.

arts.ttl: Bayern president "cautiously optimistic" of extending Kimmich's contract
arts.sum: Bayern Munich president Herbert Hainer is "cautiously optimistic" that a new contract can be agreed with Joshua Kimmich.

arts.ttl: Jota says Celtic fans' actions before games is 'something no-one has ever given to me'
arts.sum: That was proven on Saturday afternoon when Celtic beat Dundee United 3-0 and Jota scored his first goal at home since re-joining the club. I think Celtic fans always make me feel so well and so happy.
